MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kyle J. Clawson of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insulation of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward L. Lee

RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

At the Murphy (#274695) hearing the Court granted parties' request to also argue the same motions in the Kell (#275638), Lee (#275898), and Kase (#275958) cases. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as to the Lee case: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on August 15, 2012 and the default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 2, 2014 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Amber R. Craig of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendants / moving parties, Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. F/K/A Kaiser Cement Corporation and Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward L. Lee

RULING:
DEFENDANTS HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION'S AND KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.'s and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendants failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendants failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Defendants failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendants acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendants' special interrogatories preclude defendants from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to sign in sheet for other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO MAINTAIN THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A), (D)

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's motion to maintain the current trial date with preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a), (d) is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 36(a) because he failed to demonstrate that his health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation. Plaintiff failed to meet the requirements of C.C.P. 36(d) because he failed to submit clear and convincing medical documentation raising substantial medical doubt as to his survival beyond six months. Dr. Barrow's declaration is insufficient to meet this burden. The Court will set this case on an expedited trial date August 5, 2013. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
